prompt:
Anatomical muscle-activation diagram of the "俯卧 Y 字" (prone Y raise) exercise, view from above and slightly behind. Person posture: Athletic person doing a prone Y: lying face-down on the floor, legs extended; both arms extended forward and angled outward at about 45 degrees from the body so the arms form the letter Y from above; thumbs up, palms facing each other; arms lifted off the ground a few inches by squeezing the upper back; this targets the lower trapezius emphasizing scapular depression; plain black athletic shorts and gray tank top. Body rendered with semi-transparent skin so underlying muscles are visible. Highlight in saturated red the primary working muscles: lower trapezius and rear deltoid. Highlight in orange the secondary stabilizers: rhomboids and erector spinae. Other muscles in pale neutral gray. Add small clean labels with thin black arrow leader lines pointing from each label to its target muscle group. The labels MUST be in simplified Chinese characters using a clean sans-serif font. Use exactly the following labels: "下斜方肌" pointing to the lower trapezius; "三角肌后束" pointing to the rear deltoid; "菱形肌 / 竖脊肌" pointing to the rhomboids and erector spinae. 

CRITICAL CONSTRAINTS — these are non-negotiable:
1. ALL TEXT IN THE IMAGE MUST BE SIMPLIFIED CHINESE CHARACTERS ONLY.
2. ABSOLUTELY NO ENGLISH WORDS, NO LATIN LETTERS, NO PINYIN ANYWHERE IN THE IMAGE.
3. NO romanization or transliteration of muscle names.
4. NO captions, NO subtitles, NO step-by-step text overlays.
5. ONLY the muscle labels listed above should appear as text.

Style: clean medical textbook illustration, off-white background, no facial features (head shape may be shown but no eyes/nose/mouth), anatomically accurate proportions, no shadows on background, no watermarks, no signature.
